name: SignalBrief Social Intel
description:
Real-time social mention intelligence for any brand, competitor, or keyword. Scans Hacker News stories and comments to produce a structured brief with sentiment analysis, theme clustering, urgency-ranked highlights, and actionable recommendations.

Features:
- Scans Hacker News stories and comments in real time via the Algolia API
- Engagement-weighted sentiment analysis (positive, neutral, negative)
- Theme clustering into six categories: pricing_complaints, support_issues, feature_requests, switching_intent, praise, general_discussion
- Urgency scoring (0-10) combining sentiment signals, churn language, and engagement metrics
- Top 5 most important mentions with direct source links and one-line explanations
- Actionable recommendation based on dominant themes and overall sentiment
- Configurable time windows: 24h, 7d, 30d

Try asking:
- "What are people saying about Stripe on Hacker News this week?"
- "Get me a social brief for Notion over the last 24 hours"
- "What's the sentiment around Figma in the last 7 days?"
- "Are there any urgent mentions about Linear in the last 30 days?"
- "Show me switching intent signals for Slack from the past week"
- "What feature requests are people making about Vercel on Hacker News?"
- "Give me a full social intelligence brief for OpenAI with urgency-ranked mentions"

Agent tips:
- Use the 7d window (default) for a balanced view; 24h for breaking issues; 30d for trend analysis
- The tool returns both human-readable text (summary + recommendation) and full structured data via structuredContent
- Theme categories map directly to actionable business responses (e.g. switching_intent signals churn risk)
- Rate limited to ~10 requests/minute due to upstream API constraints
